name: Indian Oil Marketing (IOC/BPCL/HPCL)
summary: Day 78. **BRENT $109.26 +3.3% 1D / WTI $105.42 +4.2% â€” OMC RELIEF ENVELOPE NOW FULLY OUT-OF-RANGE; structural-disruption frame reasserting through WTI +11% weekly. Hardeep Puri â‚ą1,000 cr/day PSU OMC under-recovery (â‚ą1.98 lakh cr on track) is the operative fiscal-pressure framing.** **A4 MODI-MBZ MoUs SIGNED MAY 15 with long-term LPG SALE-AND-PURCHASE FRAMEWORK BETWEEN INDIANOIL AND ADNOC** (CONFIRMED â€” Business Standard, The Tribune, The Wire) = structural India-LPG architecture cemented; provides operational hedge against Hormuz disruption for IOC specifically. **B7 nationwide fuel price hike (reported May 15)** â€” politically-explosive pass-through that yesterday's brief flagged as trigger condition is firing. PSU OMC equity overhang persists; Reliance refining-margin tailwind diverges from OMC marketing-margin compression. **The MOU framework as structural relief catalyst remains operative**: would require Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions + Hormuz operational reopening. Neither imminent. PRIOR (May 7): **OMC RELIEF ENVELOPE NOW IN-RANGE: BRENT AT $101.95 MAY 7 MORNING (-7% MAY 6 ON MOU SCOOP); WORLD BANK $86 AVERAGE FOR 2026 + GOLDMAN Q2 $90 = INSTITUTIONAL FORECAST BAND BELOW INDIAN OMC LOSS-THRESHOLD.** Brent at $101 compresses CAD pressure significantly vs $115 â€” but OMCs remain capped because (1) Hormuz still operationally closed (5-6 vessels/day vs 138 norm; Trump pause preserved blockade explicitly); (2) $101 still above India's structural-loss-threshold (~$85-90 ceiling without pump-price pass-through); (3) Iran's first unconditional Hormuz reopening offer of cycle is REPORTED (PBS/AP/Al Jazeera/IRNA) but NOT yet operationally translated. The OMC forced-choice 30-day window (started ~Apr 13) is at Day ~24, expiring ~May 27. RBI special-credit-facility for OMC USD-demand REMAINS ACTIVE. CHABAHAR remains closed (US waiver expired April 26). **The MOU framework is the structural relief catalyst:** if signed within 14 days (by May 21) AND Hormuz operationally reopens (transit volume above 30/day for 3 consecutive days), OMC compressed-spread trade unwinds â€” Brent toward $86-90 (Goldman/World Bank) materially improves IOC/BPCL/HPCL margin profile. Watch Araghchi-India May 7-8 for any signal on India-Iran rupee-settlement oil track restart (would re-open the Chabahar escape hatch).
